title: Support specifying dependencies for `FROM oci-archive:` more cleanly
body:
Right now when using `FROM $localtransport:$path` (such as `FROM oci-archive:foo.tar`), where the local artifact is built by a previous stage, one has to trick buildah into ensuring that the previous stage actually runs before the target stage. A common way to do this is to use a `RUN`/`COPY` directive in the final stage which references the previous stage:

```Dockerfile
FROM oci-archive:./out.ociarchive
RUN --mount=type=bind,from=builder,target=/var/tmp true
```

See e.g. a real Containerfile example [here](https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-config/blob/459b2a406df7354fa44056c97c67efbd33b0f273/Containerfile#L59-L63).

The main issue with this in the general case is that it breaks with `--jobs N` for N != 1 because the `FROM` may end up being "pulled" before it's actually ready. A seemingly cosmetic issue is that it causes an empty rootfs layer to be appended. Though it does also mean that it's not possible for the previous stage to fully dictate the final build artifact, which is a desirable property.

Another approach I found out recently is to use `--skip-unused-stages=false`. It fixes the cosmetic issue, but it would require users/CI/build systems to pass that flag when building. It can also still break with `--jobs N` for N != 1 and obviously may end up causing unnecessary stages to be built. It works well though for the common case, which is when your stages are linear and all depend on each other (and you're not e.g. defining multiple targets and using `--target`).

So anyway, while these workarounds more or less work for now, I think it'd still be nice to have better support for this in buildah by somehow making this dependency explicit.

See also https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5952 in which various options for how to do this have been discussed.

comments.body:
Chatting with @nalind, he said:


> nalind: syntactically, a `--after=` flag that could point to another stage would probably be enough.
> nalind: (as a FROM flag)


which seems fine to me.

Going to hack on this if someone wants to assign it to me.
